mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate signals accommodative monetary policy, compressing discount rates and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate environments are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation expectations, indicated by a 0.5% monthly increase in CPIAUCSL, prompt central bank tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite inflation spikes, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payroll employment (PAYEMS > 130k) and declining unemployment (UNRATE < 7%) signal a tightening labor market, boosting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX returns in triggered scenarios show negative or neutral correlation with combined PAYEMS/UNRATE signals, or interaction effect size < 0.15 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A housing market boom, indicated by HOUST > 900k units, reflects strong consumer demand and wealth effects, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns in high-HOUST environments show negative or neutral correlation, or 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered cases are negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO > 100) signals robust supply chain activity, reducing recession risks and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns in high-INDPRO environments show negative or neutral correlation, or 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered cases are negative.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1 > 15000 billion) reflects resilient demand, supporting equity valuations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns in high-GDPC1 environments show negative or neutral correlation, or 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered cases are negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 0.5% in 1 month) signals tightening monetary policy, increasing discount rates and driving negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rate hikes, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: High unemployment (UNRATE > 7.5%) signals weak labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending and driving negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns in high-UNRATE environments show positive or neutral correlation, or 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered cases are positive.
